title: Assam’s dramatis personae: Himanta, Gaurav, ‘Miyas’ and also development
content: The BJP’s pet narrative of protecting Assam from the ‘infiltrators’, and the personal rivalry between Himanta Biswa Sarma and Gaurav Gogoi are likely to dominate Assam’s first single-phase Assembly polls in 25 years

title: MDMK calls for total prohibition and life imprisonment for sexual offenders
content: In its manifesto released on Tuesday (March 17, 2026) by party general secretary Vaiko, the MDMK said Tamil Nadu should be free from liquor and other drugs which are ‘responsible for the degeneration of youth’

title: March taken out to Lok Bhavan in protest against Bill on transgender persons
content: Copies of the Bill burnt as members of transgender, intersex, and gender diverse community demand its withdrawal; they allege that it takes away the right to gender self-determination and violates human and constitutional rights

title: Majority of employment creation in agriculture, says report
content: The country is nearing the peak of its demographic dividend, with the share of the working age population expected to begin declining after 2030, says the report

title: Government plans natural farming in over 61,000 acres from kharif
content: Natural farming would be implemented in 489 clusters with a cost of ₹42 crore and the farmers selected for the mission would be first trained at some identified Rythu Vedikas

title: SEC pushes live webcasting from all polling booths during GBA polls, FD turns down proposal
content: G.S. Sangreshi pointed to the 2025 Bihar assembly elections, where Election Commission of India ensured live webcasting from all polling booths.

title: Tiger ‘Explorer’ walks back to Papikonda National Park
content: It was inching closer to Yeleru reservoir from the Pampa reservoir through dense forest cover in the Kakinada district by Tuesday night.

title: Karnataka High Court refuses to quash rape case against government school teacher for repeated abuse and blackmail of colleague
content: The allegations made in the complaint and the charge-sheet depict “not consent but submission extracted under duress” and that the petitioner must face a full-fledged trial, said Justice M. Nagaprasanna

title: Switching to electric power has saved Railways ₹6,000 crore: Vaishnaw in Lok Sabha
content: Responding to a debate on demands for grants of the Railway Ministry, he also said that the national transporter has become the second largest cargo carrier in the world.

title: Indian Navy joins U.S.-led drill ‘Sea Dragon’ in Guam
content: The exercise brings together forces from Australia, Japan and New Zealand, aimed at enhancing coordination and combat readiness in undersea warfare

title: Urban Local Bodies in A.P. to get smart streetlighting system
content: The smart network to enable real-time monitoring, centralised control, automated fault detection and predictive maintenance, improving reliability of public lighting while lowering power usage

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Congress announces first list of 55 candidates, yet to take a call on MPs wishing to contest
content: K.C. Venugopal says party will take a decision on MPs' desire to contest later. Party has not yet named candidates for Kannur and Konni for which MPs K. Sudhakaran and Adoor Prakash respectively raised demands. The initial list includes 20 incumbent MLAs

title: Leela Samson pays ₹8 lakh for terming dance intern as ‘mistress’ in her Facebook post, gets criminal case quashed
content: Justice M. Nirmal Kumar of the Madras High Court quashes the criminal case since the complainant was not inclined to prosecute the matter any further

title: Farmers are saving ₹25,000 through the paddy bonus: Bhatti
content: ₹57,000 crore in interest-free loans provided to women, says Deputy Chief Minister; the previous regime failed to implement key promises like double-bedroom houses, one job per household, a Dalit Chief Minister, and land distribution to the landless poor even after ten years in power, says Bhatti

title: Huge devotee turnout for Ugadi Mahotsavams in Srisailam
content: Executive Officer says that a large number of devotees from Karnataka and Maharashtra’s Sangli, Solapur, and other parts are reaching the temple town by foot passing through the Nallamala forests

title: FIR against ‘Mohammed’ Deepak: Uttarakhand High Court seeks status report
content: The High Court also asked the police to clarify why no case had been registered based on Mr. Deepak’s complaint against individuals who were clearly identifiable in the video recordings.

title: With exports halting due to Gulf war, banana farmers in Andhra Pradesh look to govt. support
content: Prices that ranged between ₹22,000 and ₹25,000 per tonne have now crashed to around ₹5,000, leaving farmers in distress, says Andhra Pradesh Rythu Sangham in a representation to Agriculture Minister Atchannaidu, seeking urgent government intervention

title: FAC allows KFDC to continue tree felling operations
content: The MoEFCC has approved the continued removal of exotic species, such as eucalyptus, wattle, and acacia

title: Govt. sanctions land for CUK Capital Centre in Thiruvananthapuram
content: Land has been allotted in Andoorkonam village to establish a permanent campus of the Central University of Kerala, in the district

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Former KPCC general secretary M.J. Job joins BJP
content: He had quit the party over its decision to support former CPI(M) leader G. Sudhakaran in the Ambalapuzha constituency

title: AIR Vijayawada to broadcast special literary programme for Ugadi
content: The 59-minute programme will be broadcast on March 19 at 9.30 a.m. from the Vijayawada station and will be repeated at 10.30 a.m. on FM Rainbow 102.2., says programme head

title: No evidence of increased efficiency due to mechanisation of sewer cleaning, Centre tells LS
content: Centre says 622 deaths from hazardous cleaning reported since 2017, 842 complaints in 2025 over denial of wages, no protective gear, and caste bias; insists that sewer and septic tank cleaning work is “occupation-based”, not caste-based

title: Bengaluru receives first pre-monsoon showers
content: IMD officials said that the city will continue to receive light rainfall for the next two to three days

title: ECI deploys over 1,000 Central observers for upcoming Assembly polls
content: West Bengal has been assigned highest number—one general observer for each of 294 constituencies, along with 84 police observers and 100 expenditure observers.

title: Police intensify probe into Kowdiar Palace theft
content: Investigators compile list of all visitors who entered the palace between October and November, last year; they suspect the theft was carried out by someone familiar with the palace layout

title: A.P. BJP chief urges government to allocate funds for major festivals
content: Party has launched a special initiative titled ‘Janata Varadhi’ to strengthen coordination between the public and the government, says P.V.N. Madhav

title: West Asia conflict: Sharp drop in flight services to Dubai
content: Following recurring UAE airspace restrictions, triggered by continued retaliatory strikes by Iran on US bases in the Gulf

title: ‘Koil Alwar Thirumanjanam’ performed in Tirumala ahead of Ugadi
content: The traditional temple cleansing ritual has been performed in strict adherence to the Agama scriptures

title: A.P. govt. announces one-time 50% interest waiver on property tax arrears
content: Taxpayers can avail of the benefit by paying 100% of principal property tax dues along with 50% of interest in a single lump sum payment until March 31, 2026

title: Rayalaseema leaders seek immediate water release from Srisailam Dam
content: Submitting a representation to district Collector Rajakumari; emphasise that decisions on water release must align with existing GOs, Bachawat Tribunal provisions, and the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act

title: Free travel scheme for differently-abled persons to be launched in A.P. on March 18
content: People with 40% or more disability can travel free of cost in APSRTC services, including city ordinary, metro express, Palle Velugu, Ultra-Palle Velugu, and express buses

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: BJP suspends dissident leader in Kottayam
content: Noble Mathew, national vice-president of Minority Morcha, has been suspended from the  primary membership of the party citing ‘anti-organisational activities’

title: Govt. sets May 15 as deadline for completion of all irrigation O&M works in Andhra Pradesh
content: Basic maintenance measures such as greasing equipment too have been ignored by the YSRCP government, alleges Water Resources Minister Nimmala Ramanaidu, and directs officials to undertake the works before the commencement of the 2026 Kharif season

title: Vijay, TVK seniors eye Chennai constituencies
content: Vijay is highly likely to make his electoral debut from the Perambur constituency, sources in the party say

title: 39,349 students to appear for SSLC exam in Mysuru district
content: All necessary arrangements have been made at 136 examination centres, including 43 centres in Mysuru city alone

title: RDSS extends financial aid to families of 17 farmer suicide victims in Vijayawada
content: Each family given ₹50,000 to help rebuild their lives through small income-generating activities such as dairy farming, livestock rearing, or tailoring, says RDSS secretary

title: 14 arrested in Varanasi on BJP Yuva Morcha's complaint after hosting Iftar on Ganga
content: Police said that a video went viral on March 16, purportedly showing a group of people holding an Iftar party on a boat on the Ganga

title: Creating 20 lakh jobs is the government’s single-point agenda, says HRD Minister Lokesh
content: Urging the officials to ensure that the investment agreements translate into tangible projects, he says MoUs alone are not enough, and they must lead to real investments and employment generation

title: Kiper's new NFL mock draft: How free agency shook up first-round landing spots for top prospects
content: Team needs have shifted in free agency, so how will top prospects come off the board in Round 1? Mel Kiper Jr. projects the NFL draft's first 32 picks.

title: Source: Broncos get Waddle in deal with Dolphins
content: The Broncos are trading for Dolphins wide receiver Jaylen Waddle in exchange for first-, third- and fourth-round picks in this year's draft, a source told ESPN.

title: Boozer among freshmen on All-America 1st team
content: Duke forward Cameron Boozer is a unanimous first-team All-American in college basketball in voting released by The Associated Press.

title: Bama preps to play without Holloway after arrest
content: Alabama coach Nate Oats said the team is preparing to play without star Aden Holloway in the NCAA tournament following Holloway's arrest Monday morning on a felony drug charge.

title: KD says loss to Lakers on him: 'I'm the offense'
content: The Lakers, in the process of carving out a defensive identity, threw a steady diet of double-teams at Kevin Durant in the fourth quarter Monday, leading to disastrous results for Houston. "I just felt like I lost the game for us tonight," said Durant.

title: Hawks fans rep Magic City despite nixed promo
content: Hawks fans showed up in Magic City gear Monday night even though the NBA shut down the team's short-lived promotion with the well-known strip club.

title: U-M's Whittingham looks to refine QB Underwood
content: In his first year at Michigan, coach Kyle Whittingham looks to develop sophomore quarterback Bryce Underwood into a "completely finished product" and improve the Wolverines' struggling passing game.

title: Pulisic, McKennie headline March USMNT roster
content: AC Milan attacker Christian Pulisic, Juventus midfielder Weston McKennie and Crystal Palace center back Chris Richards headline the 27-player roster named by United States men's national manager Mauricio Pochettino for the upcoming international window.

content: Which double-digit seeds have the highest potential to advance to the second round? Here's what ESPN's BPI says.

content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

content: Who will win Tuesday's World Baseball Classic title game? We have you covered with predictions, live updates and takeaways.

content: One must-see guard scores from anywhere like the WNBA's Kelsey Plum. Another handles pressure like Chelsea Gray.

content: The NBA seems closer than ever to adding two franchises. Here's what we know, including how an expansion draft could work.

content: A wild card or Pacific Division spot both remain possible. Plus, full playoff projections, draft lottery standings, more.

content: Will any of the wild cards make a run? Plus, full playoff projections, draft lottery standings, more.

title: A pair of holes-in-one lead the best moments from TGL season
content: Neal Shipley and Tom Kim recorded the only holes-in-one in TGL history during the 2026 regular season.

title: Real or Not: These 8 MLB stars can reach new heights in 2026
content: Another record for Shohei Ohtani? A 40/40 season for Juan Soto? We hand out preseason Real or Not verdicts.

title: Portland goes No. 1 in 2026's first week
content: With the first matchday of the 2026 season in the books, ESPN ranks all 16 NWSL clubs from first to worst in this week's edition of Power Rankings.

title: Liverpool's most pivotal match of the season is up next -- and Slot's job may depend on it
content: As Liverpool continue to unravel, this week's Champions League fixture against Galatasaray is make-or-break for Slot's standing at the club.

title: ESPN's final points-league mock draft for 2026
content: Check out the results of our final fantasy baseball head-to-head points mock draft of 2026, featuring round-by-round picks and a snapshot of the rosters of all 10 teams.

title: Pickups: Look to Jazz, Trail Blazers for waiver-wire options
content: Every week in fantasy basketball, there are available players who can help fill holes in your roster. Here are the ones to add for the week ahead.

title: MLS Power Rankings: LAFC off to a record-breaking start
content: LAFC have already made history with their lockdown defense, but are they No. 1? Here's this week's edition of ESPN's MLS Power Rankings.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United outline preferred midfield targets
content: Manchester United have outlined their summer transfer targets, while another European giant has entered the race for out-of-contract midfielder Leon Goretzka.

title: 'We don't need help!' Trump explodes after Nato, other nations refuse to take part in Iran war
content: US President Donald Trump declared America's self-sufficiency in its military operations against Iran, stating the nation is the world's most powerful and requires no allied support. He noted that many Nato allies declined to join the campaign, which he views as a one-way street. Trump also claimed significant military successes against Iran.

title: 'I am sorry': How Suryakumar Yadav defused Arshdeep Singh-Daryl Mitchell scuffle
content: India T20 captain Suryakumar Yadav apologised to Daryl Mitchell after Arshdeep Singh accidentally hit him with a throw in the World Cup final. Arshdeep was fined but later apologised. Suryakumar said such moments happen in heat but stressed leadership and sportsmanship, adding he said sorry regardless of intent to set the right example.

title: 'Iran posed no threat': Trump's counterterror chief resigns, cites Israel 'pressure'
content: A top counterterrorism official, Joe Kent, resigned, stating Iran posed no imminent threat and the US-Iran conflict was driven by Israeli pressure and misinformation. Kent, who served as director of the National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC), announced his decision saying, after much reflection, I have decided to resign from my position effective today.

title: OpenAI signs new deal, to bring its AI to US military through Amazon
content: OpenAI has secured a significant agreement to provide its AI models to US defense and government agencies via AWS, enabling access for both classified and unclassified operations. This move follows rival Anthropic's exclusion due to supply chain concerns, leaving a void OpenAI is poised to fill. The deal leverages AWS's existing federal infrastructure and includes a substantial investment from Amazon.

title: Beyond oil & gas: How Iran war impacts India - from diamonds to fertilisers
content: Beyond oil and gas, several other sectors of the economy will likely see a hit if the US-Iran war continues for a longer duration. According to Barclays, India's non-energy imports from the Middle East are sizable as well - at approximately 10% of total non-energy imports.

title: 'Don't play just for match-ups': Ex-coach wants this PBKS bowler to play 'every match'
content: Former India cricketer Sanjay Bangar advocates for Harpreet Brar's inclusion in every Punjab Kings match this season, citing his effectiveness against all batters and ability to provide crucial powerplay overs. Bangar also expressed confidence in the opening partnership of Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya, though he noted Arya's recent domestic form could be a factor.

title: Zerodha founder Nithin Kamath says: I don't use internet banking apps on my phone because ...
content: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath revealed he avoids banking apps due to their invasive permissions, questioning why they need access to SMS, contacts, and calls. He contrasted this with Zerodha's Kite app, which requests zero permissions, highlighting the Principle of Least Privilege and SEBI's strong two-factor authentication as enabling security without intrusive data collection.

title: Passive euthanasia: Harish Rana case revived Aruna Shanbaug’s long fight for dignity in death
content: The Supreme Court has granted approval for passive euthanasia for Harish Rana, a 32-year-old man in a vegetative state for over 13 years. This historic verdict allows the withdrawal of life support, offering a dignified death and ending his family's prolonged agony. The decision follows recommendations from medical boards and personal interaction with Rana's parents.

title: Argentina formally exits WHO, following US withdrawal earlier this year
content: Argentina has officially exited the World Health Organization, following a year-long announcement by President Javier Milei's administration. Citing the WHO's alleged mishandling of the Covid-19 pandemic and political motivations over science, Buenos Aires has completed its withdrawal. This move mirrors the United States' earlier departure and raises concerns about global health coordination.

title: America’s largest medical device maker Stryker ‘back’ 6 days after cyber attack
content: America's largest medical device maker, Stryker, has contained a cyberattack. The incident disrupted global operations for nearly a week. The company is now focused on restoring ordering, shipping, and customer services. No medical products or patient services were compromised during the cyber incident. Stryker is working to bring its electronic ordering systems back online.

title: Over $100mn per day: How global oil shock has turned into a jackpot for Iran and Russia
content: A US-Iran war is causing a dangerous oil shock, ironically enriching both Tehran and Moscow. As Gulf energy assets and shipping are disrupted, crude prices surge, providing a revenue windfall for Iran and benefiting Russia through tighter global supply and renewed Asian demand.

title: Nana Patekar says only Amitabh Bachchan and he call actors directly
content: Veteran actor Nana Patekar and director Prakash Jha have voiced concerns about the growing complexity and cost associated with working with big stars. They highlighted how unreachable stars and their large entourages, managed by corporate structures, inflate film budgets, making direct communication and creative collaboration increasingly difficult.

title: Matosevic handed four-year suspension after doping admission, ITIA acts
content: Marinko Matosevic has been banned for four years after being found guilty of multiple doping violations. The International Tennis Integrity Agency confirmed the decision, which also includes returning prize money. While Matosevic admitted to some actions and criticised the process, the tribunal rejected his claims and called his conduct a serious breach of tennis rules.

title: Lions emerge as surprise destination for Cameron Jordan in NFL trade buzz
content: Cameron Jordan still wants to feel valued, and that has put his Saints future in real doubt. After a 10.5-sack season in 2025, the veteran edge rusher remains unsigned. The Detroit Lions have been floated as a possible fit, but New Orleans is not out yet. Here’s why this rumor has real traction and what it means now.

title: Iran vs US tensions cloud FIFA World Cup plans, matches may shift to Mexico
content: Iran’s 2026 World Cup participation is uncertain amid rising tensions. The country is negotiating with FIFA to shift matches from the US to Mexico, citing safety concerns. Officials said, "We will certainly not travel to America." FIFA hasn’t agreed yet. After Donald Trump warned about safety, Iran insisted "no one can exclude" them from the tournament.

title: Exclusive - 'Action will follow. Pak's brutality won't go unanswered': Taliban
content: Taliban spokesperson Zabihullah Mujahid vowed retaliation after an alleged Pakistani airstrike on Omar Addiction Treatment Hospital in Kabul, which he described as a deliberate attack on civilians. Mujahid stated the attack "will not go unanswered" and accused Pakistan of attempting to shape the narrative, vowing to expose their "brutality" to global human rights bodies.

title: AB de Villiers tells MS Dhoni 'bat higher or be out of CSK squad'
content: Dhoni, 44, will play for CSK when the season begins on March 28. De Villiers said Dhoni’s recent trend of batting at No. 8 or 9 limits his impact.

title: 'We just wanted talks': Sonam Wangchuk's first reaction after release from jail
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, released after NSA detention, stated Ladakh's goal is dialogue with the Centre. He called his release a "win-win," enabling trust-building for meaningful talks. Wangchuk, detained for six months, expressed relief at avoiding sharing his "horror stories" from jail, hoping for constructive engagement.

title: Ranveer Singh recalls Deepika’s pain while helping Dhurandhar co-star
content: Rajat Arora got injured filming an action scene on 'Dhurandhar' set, playing Mukka in Aditya Dhar's film. As he fell, Ranveer Singh rushed to lift him, relating via Deepika Padukone's similar pain. Ranveer took him to first-aid, teaching resilience. Rajat shared learnings: actor bonding, character backstory depth, and creative surroundings use for impactful shots.

title: LPG crisis hits IT firms; canteens cut, staff asked to bring lunch
content: A cooking gas crunch, stemming from the West Asia conflict, has forced major Indian IT firms like Infosys, TCS, and Cognizant to drastically cut canteen services. Employees are now compelled to bring packed lunches, impacting thousands who rely on office cafeterias, especially those in PGs and hostels with limited options.

title: RS polls: Cong alleges BJP tried ‘vote chori’ in Haryana, warns cross-voters of strict action
content: The Congress accused the BJP of "vote chori" and "murdering democracy" in Haryana's Rajya Sabha elections after cross-voting and invalid ballots marred the contest. Despite acknowledging internal cross-voting and promising strict action against its MLAs, the Congress secured one seat, while the BJP won the other. The outcome has ignited a political battle and an internal party crisis.

title: Smith, Warner’s PSL plans under scanner amid ‘do not travel’ alert
content: Steve Smith and David Warner face uncertainty over joining the Pakistan Super League due to rising security concerns. Tensions, travel disruptions, and a “Do Not Travel” alert for Peshawar have raised alarms. While preparations continue, players are weighing safety risks, with contingency plans like emergency evacuation under consideration before final decisions are made.

title: Why Trump's 'beautiful armada' cannot take control of the Strait of Hormuz
content: The proposal from President Trump to assemble an international naval coalition for the defense of the Strait of Hormuz encounters formidable obstacles. Iran's advanced A2/AD capabilities—featuring an array of missiles, drones, and submarines—combined with the strait's challenging topography, complicates even the strongest navy's efforts to maintain a permanent presence.

title: RCB’s Hazlewood to miss IPL 2026 start, out for two weeks
content: Josh Hazlewood will miss the start of IPL 2026 due to injury, delaying his return by nearly five months. Pat Cummins is also recovering and may join later. Meanwhile, players like Steve Smith face safety concerns over joining the Pakistan Super League amid regional tensions, with evacuation plans being considered.

title: India slams Pak for ‘fabricating’ Islamophobia narratives; flags Ahmadiyya repression, Afg bombings
content: India has accused Pakistan of fabricating Islamophobia narratives at the UN, questioning Islamabad's treatment of minorities and its actions against Afghanistan. Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish urged the UN to address all religious discrimination, not just one faith, highlighting India's large Muslim population and pluralistic ethos. He warned against weaponizing religion for political gain.

title: Exclusive - 'If I could change my body, I would': Sumit Nagal on injuries
content: India's Sumit Nagal is fighting back after a turbulent career plagued by injuries, including hip surgery and a hip flexor tear, that saw him drop from World No. 68 to No. 278. Now refocused on fitness, diet, and the controllables of his game, Nagal heads into his favourite time of the season - on clay - with hard-won maturity and renewed determination.

title: US-Iran war: How LPG cylinder has become the face of Middle East mayhem's impact on India
content: Global tensions in the Middle East are directly impacting Indian households, causing a severe LPG shortage.  Rising fuel costs and disrupted shipments have led to panic bookings, long queues, and black marketing, forcing businesses to adapt and highlighting India's heavy reliance on imports.  The situation is escalating, affecting daily life and livelihoods across the nation.

title: Salman Agha runout: Should umpires have called dead ball? MCC settles debate
content: The Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC) has confirmed a controversial run-out in the Bangladesh vs. Pakistan ODI was correctly given out under the Laws of Cricket. The non-striker was out of his ground when the wicket was broken, and the ball remained in play, making the dismissal lawful.

title: Govt steps in, instructs CBFC to take strict action against Nora Fatehi's song 'Sarke Chunar'
content: A controversial song from 'KD: The Devil', featuring Nora Fatehi and Sanjay Dutt, has drawn government intervention. Amidst allegations of vulgar lyrics and visuals, the Hindi version was removed from YouTube.

title: 'Use LPG wisely': Centre says supply 'matter of concern'; advises consumers not to panic
content: India's energy crisis persists due to Middle East conflict, but the government assures consumers about LPG supplies. While urging judicious consumption and alternate fuel use, authorities are conducting raids to curb hoarding and black marketing of LPG cylinders across several states.

title: US gas prices: Hormuz tensions push diesel above $5/gallon, highest since Dec 2022
content: Retail diesel prices in the U.S. have surpassed $5 a gallon for the second time, impacting global economic activity and consumer goods. This surge, driven by the US-Israeli conflict with Iran and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, presents a challenge for the upcoming midterm elections.  Gasoline prices also reached their highest since October 2023, with little sign of easing.

title: When is Eid-ul-Fitr 2026? Key dates, historical context and significance explained
content: Eid-ul-Fitr in India is anticipated on March 20 or 21, 2026, contingent on the moon sighting. This joyous festival marks the end of Ramadan's fasting and spiritual reflection.  It's a time for gratitude, community, and renewed faith, celebrating the successful completion of a month of discipline and devotion.

title: SC rules adoptive mothers can’t be denied maternity leave if child is older than 3 months
content: The Supreme Court has struck down a provision in the Social Security Code 2020 that limited maternity leave for adoptive mothers. The court declared the provision irrational, stating adoptive children are no different from biological ones. Consequently, adoptive mothers are now entitled to 12 weeks of maternity leave, irrespective of the child's age at adoption.

title: 'We were shocked': Trump reacts to Iran hitting US Gulf allies — was there prior warning?
content: US President Trump expressed shock at Iran's expanded strikes across the Middle East, despite US intelligence anticipating such retaliation. The conflict, now in its third week, has seen Iranian attacks on US positions and allied infrastructure, disrupting vital shipping lanes like the Strait of Hormuz.  Concerns are mounting over the war's justification and its escalating regional impact.

title: 5 IPL legends to have not won the Orange Cap
content: As IPL 2026 nears, a surprising fact emerges: several top run-scorers have never won the Orange Cap. Legends like Rohit Sharma, Shikhar Dhawan, Suresh Raina, AB de Villiers, Ajinkya Rahane, and MS Dhoni, despite their high rankings and impactful careers, have surprisingly never finished a season as the leading run-scorer.

title: Jawahar Navodaya Class 6 result out: Direct scorecard link here
content: The Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti has released the JNVST Class 6 Result 2026 for summer-bound Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas. Students who appeared in the Phase 1 entrance exam held in December 2025 can now check their qualifying status online. The selection list marks the next stage of the admission process, with shortlisted candidates required to complete document verification.

title: "Ideologically, No Place For Me In Current JD(U)": KC Tyagi To NDTV On Exit
content: KC Tyagi, a founder-member of Nitish Kumar's Janata Dal United and once the party's face in Delhi, has unplugged himself from the party, ending a 40-year association.

title: US And Ukraine's Reply After American, 6 Others Arrested Under India's Anti-Terror Law
content: The US embassy in India has said it is "aware of the situation" after the National Investigation Agency (NIA), the country's top anti-terror body, announced that it had arrested seven people, including an American national.

title: Who Is Joe Kent, Trump's Pick To Fight Terrorism Who Quit Over Iran War
content: Joesph Kent, the director of the United States' National Counterterrorism Centre, has resigned, saying he cannot support the Trump administration's military campaign against Iran and that the war was started not out of genuine necessity.

title: Maharashtra Assembly Passes Bill To Repeal 80 Obsolete Laws
content: A Bill to repeal 80 obsolete Acts and laws in Maharashtra was passed in the Legislative Assembly today, marking a significant step towards strengthening good governance and streamlining administrative processes.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Netanyahu Claims Israel Killed Top Iranian Security Chief Ali Larijani
content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: The war in the Middle East broke out when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

title: "Cannot Support Iran War In Good Conscience": Top Trump Official Resigns
content: Joseph Kent, the head of the United States' National Counterterrorism Center, has resigned from his position over the US-Israel war against Iran.

title: Who Is Matthew VanDyke, American Arrested In India Over Drone Warfare Training
content: An American citizen has landed at the centre of a major investigation by Indian security agencies, with the National Investigation Agency arresting him alongside six Ukrainian nationals in a case involving alleged drone warfare training.

title: 'What Good Is My Win If Ladakh Loses?' Sonam Wangchuk After Release From Jail
content: The innovator-activist walked out of Jodhpur Central Jail on Saturday after the Centre revoked his detention under the National Security Act (NSA).

title: Ujjain Student Killed In Canada, Family Battles Grief, Rs 50 Lakh Burden
content: To bring Gurkirat's body back to India, the family has been asked to deposit nearly $40,000 around Rs 35 lakh. The hospital procedures will take another 15 days, and transporting the body to Ujjain would cost an additional Rs 10 lakh.

title: Trinamool's Latest Line-Up Has 2 Ministers Who Ran Into 'Messi' Problem
content: The Trinamool Congress has given tickets to contest the West Bengal assembly election to two leaders who ran into a controversy over an event featuring footballer Lionel Messi in December 2025.

title: Hormuz Escape Route Suggests Ships Navigating On Iran's Terms
content: Since US and Israeli attacks began a little over two weeks ago, Iran has hit several ships in and around the strait, and all but closed the waterway.

title: Opinion: Blog | West Bengal: BJP's Grand Plan Behind Fielding Suvendu Adhikari From 2 Seats
content: By turning the contest into a direct confrontation with Mamata Banerjee, the BJP aims to sharpen the narrative, energise its base, and raise the stakes of the election battle.

title: Is Mojtaba Khamenei Recovering At A Hospital In Moscow? What Russia Said
content: Mojtaba Khamenei, the new Supreme Leader of Iran, has reportedly been transferred to Russia for medical treatment after he was severely injured in the US-Israeli airstrike that killed his father on February 28.

title: After Delhi Bridge Collapse Kills Woman, Government Orders Citywide Audit
content: Irrigation and Flood Control Minister Parvesh Verma has ordered an immediate investigation into the collapse of a foot overbridge in north Delhi's Roop Nagar on Tuesday morning.

title: Senior Assam Leader Threatens To Quit Congress Over Poll Nomination
content: Senior Congress leader and Lok Sabha MP Pradyut Bordoloi has warned the party leadership that he may resign from the Congress if Lahorighat MLA Asif Mohammad Nazar is renominated for the upcoming Assam Assembly elections.

title: Trump's Delay Of Summit With Xi Jinping Comes As A Win For China
content: US President Donald Trump's request for a delay to his summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping is a likely welcome development for Beijing, even as it threatens to inject new uncertainty into ties between the world's two largest economies.

title: Trinamool Announces Candidates For 2026 Bengal Polls: Check Full List
content: The Trinamool Congress has released its candidate list for the 2026 West Bengal Assembly Election. Party chief and Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee announced that the party will contest 291 out of 294 seats.

title: History Repeats Itself In Nandigram Where Suvendu Adhikari Faces His Ex-Aide
content: A top leader versus a former aide -- this was the script that played out in Bengal's Nandigram in the last assembly election, with dramatic results. This time, it is about a see a reprise, with an aide of the sitting MLA -- the BJP's Suvendu Adhikari

title: Trump Claims Surprise Over Iran Retaliation Against Gulf Allies Despite Warning
content: President Donald Trump was warned that attacking Iran could trigger retaliation against US Gulf allies despite his claims on Monday that Tehran's reaction came as a surprise, said a US official and two sources familiar with US intelligence reports.

title: Odds: United States favored to beat Venezuela in final
content: The  World Baseball Classic  final is set, and Team USA is a sizable favorite to take home its second title Tuesday, while Venezuela will need to overcome underdog odds once again to win its first. The Americans are -285 money-line favorites over the Venezuelans, which sports +225 odds for the WBC's championship game. The U.S. had opened the tournament as the odds-on -110 favorite, while Venezuela began at +900. Team USA had attracted over 50% of the handle to win the WBC title going into the quarterfinals at BetMGM, making the Americans the sportsbook's largest liability. Venezuela, meanwhile, garnered just 2.4% of the handle, fifth-most at the book. On the tournament MVP front, Americans  Aaron Judge  (+370),  Roman Anthony  (+600) and  Bobby Witt Jr . (+850) top the odds board, along with Venezuelan  Ronald Acuna Jr.  (+750).  Maikel Garcia  (18-1) and  Luis Arraez  (20-1) are also in the running from Venezuela. For latest odds go to  DraftKings Sportsbook

title: Iran wants WC games moved from U.S. to Mexico
